description_long: This is a small log cabin constructed primarily from horizontally stacked, rounded wooden logs with visible end cuts at the corners. The cabin features a prominent gable roof made of wooden planks, extending well beyond the walls to provide ample overhang. The front facade is open, lacking a door, and reveals a dark, single rectangular window or opening, while the sides and rear are fully enclosed with logs. The structure is elevated slightly above the ground on short wooden supports, giving it a raised appearance. The wood has a rich, medium-brown finish, and the overall design is simple and rustic, reminiscent of traditional log cabins found in rural or park settings. There are no visible decorative elements, and the cabin's form is functional and robust.
description: A rustic log cabin with a gable roof, open front, and solid log walls, finished in medium-brown wood. The structure is simple, raised on short supports, and features a single front opening.
description_view_0: The front view shows the open entrance beneath the gable roof, with visible log supports and a dark rectangular opening.
description_view_1: The left side view displays the solid log wall construction and the deep roof overhang, with no windows or doors visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a fully enclosed log wall with no openings, emphasizing the cabin's solid construction and raised base.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the log wall and roof overhang, again with no windows or doors.
attribute_roof_type: gable roof with extended overhangs, constructed from wooden planks
attribute_window_arrangement: single large rectangular opening on the front facade, no windows visible on sides or rear
attribute_door_placement: no exterior doors visible; open entrance centered on the front facade
